name: Bob Cooper
abstract: Bob Cooper (December 6, 1925 – August 5, 1993) was a West Coast jazz musician known primarily for playing tenor saxophone, but also for being one of the first to play jazz solos on the oboe.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Porrello
about_quotes.evidence: in that band that I played with that was Maynard and Buddy Childers and Conte Candoli — all the heavyweights you know — Bob Cooper, Art Pepper, all those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Porrello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: VSOP put out an album of Bob Cooper and I. It was a live broadcast we did for KLON... And it was Bob Cooper’s last music before he died

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: Afro-Cuban Influences, the big band of Shorty Rogers, featuring the Giants: ... Bill Holman, ... Bob Cooper
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: Vince Guaraldi... was working at The Lighthouse with Frank Rosolino and Conte and Bob Cooper and Bud Shank... I found this place to live... I didn't know anyone
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: We were on a ship, Carl Fontana and him and Pete, his brother Pete, and Bob Cooper was alive then, Bob was playing, myself, Randy Sandke, Ross Tompkins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Holman
about_quotes.evidence: a lot of people left, Bob Cooper and Bud Shank and Shelly Manne and Shorty Rogers... So when he reorganized the band in early ’52 that’s when I joined.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Holman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999
